findings.title: North Star Metric: Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration
findings.description: The North Star metric for CollabDocs Real-time Editor is the Daily Active Users (DAU) engaged in collaboration sessions. This metric reflects the core value delivered to users through real-time collaboration features.
assumptions: Users will engage more with the product as real-time collaboration features are introduced.
recommendations: Monitor DAU closely post-launch of collaboration features.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of active users in collaboration sessions

findings.title: Primary KPI: Daily Collaboration Sessions
findings.description: This primary KPI measures the number of collaboration sessions initiated daily, indicating user engagement with the real-time editing features.
assumptions: Increased collaboration features will lead to higher session counts.
recommendations: Aim to increase daily collaboration sessions by 20% within the first quarter post-launch.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Concurrent Edit Attempts Blocked
findings.description: This guardrail metric tracks the number of concurrent edit attempts that are blocked, which should not increase as collaboration features are implemented.
assumptions: The implementation of real-time collaboration will not lead to increased blocking of edit attempts.
recommendations: Ensure that the number of blocked attempts does not exceed 2500 post-launch.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of blocked edit attempts

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Feedback on Collaboration Features
findings.description: There is currently insufficient data on user satisfaction and feedback specifically regarding the new collaboration features. This gap needs to be addressed to ensure the features meet user needs.
assumptions: User feedback will provide insights into the effectiveness of the new features.
recommendations: Implement a user feedback mechanism post-launch to gather insights on collaboration features.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing Collaboration Features
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different implementations of the real-time collaboration features to determine which version yields higher user engagement.
assumptions: Different implementations of collaboration features will have varying impacts on user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers asking for simultaneous editing

recommendations: Set up A/B tests with a minimum sample size of 1000 users per variant.

summary: The analysis identifies a North Star metric focused on daily active users engaged in collaboration, along with primary KPIs and guardrail metrics to ensure feature success. Gaps in user feedback and opportunities for A/B testing are highlighted to enhance data-driven decision-making.
